title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s are frustrating, especially when permissions haven't changed.

To help narrow this down: have you noticed any pattern to when the 403 occurs? For example:

1. **After being idle** — it works when you first log in but fails after some period of inactivity
2. **On refresh** — it fails on some page loads but works if you immediately refresh
3. **Time-based** — it tends to happen at certain times of day or during high-traffic periods
4. **Action-based** — it happens when you do something specific (e.g., switching projects, opening a particular view)
5. **Something else**

Even a rough sense of the pattern would help us figure out where to look.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It's closest to option 2 I think. Like, I'll get a 403, and if I just refresh the page it usually works after a couple tries. It doesn't seem to be related to being idle or time of day — it happens pretty much any time I use the app. I'd say maybe 1 out of every 3 page loads gives me the 403? I assumed it was some kind of network issue on our end but my coworker who also just got set up with the new analyst role is seeing the same thing.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — the refresh behavior suggests something flaky in authorization rather than a hard permissions block.

You mentioned your coworker was recently set up with the **new analyst role**. Quick question: are the team members who are **not** seeing 403s on a different role (e.g., admin, editor), or are they also on the analyst role?

This would help us figure out whether the issue is specific to that role's permission configuration.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Good question — yeah, now that I think about it, the people on my team who aren't having issues are editors and admins. It's me and one other person who both got switched to the analyst role recently, and we're both seeing it. I didn't connect those dots until you asked. Could it be something with how the analyst role is set up?

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors for users with the new analyst role","problem":"Users assigned the recently created 'analyst' role experience 403 Forbidden errors on approximately 1 in 3 dashboard page loads. Refreshing the page typically resolves the error after a couple of attempts. The issue affects all known analyst-role users and does not affect users with editor or admin roles. The problem began a few days ago, coinciding with when the analyst role was introduced.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The analyst role's permissions are likely subject to a caching or eventual-consistency issue in the authorization layer. Possible causes: (1) a permission cache that intermittently serves stale/empty permission sets for the new role, (2) a load-balanced setup where not all instances have consistent role definitions, or (3) a race condition where the role's permissions are resolved asynchronously and sometimes not ready before the authorization check runs.","reproduction_steps":["Assign a user account the 'analyst' role","Log in and navigate to the TaskFlow dashboard","Load or refresh dashboard pages repeatedly (~10-15 times)","Observe that roughly 1 in 3 loads returns a 403 Forbidden error","Confirm that refreshing after a 403 typically succeeds within 1-2 attempts"],"environment":"Affects the TaskFlow dashboard in production. Observed by multiple users on the analyst role. No specific browser, OS, or network dependency identified — the issue is server-side.","severity":"high","impact":"All users with the analyst role are unable to reliably access the dashboard, making the role effectively broken. This blocks any workflow that depends on analyst-level access.","recommended_fix":"Investigate the authorization path for the analyst role: (1) Check how role permissions are loaded and cached — look for cache TTL issues or lazy-loading race conditions specific to newly created roles. (2) Compare the analyst role's permission definition with editor/admin to see if it's structured differently or missing a required field. (3) Check whether authorization is evaluated across multiple services or instances and whether the role definition is consistently propagated. (4) Review any recent changes to the role/permission system deployed around the same time the analyst role was created.","proposed_test_case":"Create an integration test that assigns a user the analyst role and performs 50 consecutive authenticated requests to the dashboard endpoint, asserting that all return 200. Run this against a single instance and then against the load-balanced setup to isolate whether the inconsistency is per-instance or cross-instance.","information_gaps":["Exact deployment date/commit that introduced the analyst role","Whether the 403s occur on all dashboard routes or specific ones","Server-side logs showing what the authorization check returns during a 403 for an analyst user"]}
